ground_truth.description: Photo of National Basilica of the Sacred Heart in Koekelberg, Brussels. Taken from nearby park, slightly covered by trees but roof and middle facade clearly visible.
ground_truth.photographer_description: Photo of the National Basilica of the Sacred Heart in Koekelberg, Brussels. It is taken from the park nearby and is slightly covered by trees on the side, but the roof and middle part of the facade is clearly visible. It should definitely get this with metadata, and hopefully without. Daytime, summer, cloudy.

details.description: A long, formal park axis lined with tall, closely planted trees leads the eye to a massive Art Deco basilica with a prominent copper-green central dome and two flanking towers. The church’s façade is brick with pale stone trim, vertical ribs, large arched entryways, and a drum supporting the dome. Symmetrical pathways with historic-style lampposts and clipped hedges frame a broad lawn in the foreground. The setting is a grand urban park under an overcast sky, giving soft, even light. The composition centers the basilica at the vanishing point of the tree-lined allée, characteristic of the vista across Parc Elisabeth toward Koekelberg’s basilica.

entities.notes: Also called Basilique Nationale du Sacré-Cœur; one of the world’s largest Art Deco churches; copper-clad dome; construction 1905–1970; principal architect Albert Van Huffel.

details.description: A formal, tree-lined park leads straight to a monumental church at the far end. The path is flanked by tall, closely trimmed green trees and low hedges, with a central grassy strip and a few vintage-style lampposts visible along the sides. In the distance, a large, imposing building dominates the skyline: a grand basilica with a massive copper-green dome and a Byzantine-influenced façade featuring arched windows and an elevated, stepped entrance. The overall mood is serene and orderly, under a softly overcast sky that diffuses the light across the scene. The composition creates a dramatic perspective, drawing the eye along the axial lawn toward the domed basilica.

details.description: A symmetrical, eye-level view looking down a long, tree-lined avenue within a park towards a monumental religious building in the distance. The foreground features a grassy lawn and manicured hedges, flanked on both sides by dense rows of tall, mature deciduous trees that create a framing effect. At the focal point stands the National Basilica of the Sacred Heart, a massive Art Deco structure characterized by its reddish-brown brick and stone facade, vertical geometric lines, and a large, central green copper dome topped with a cross. The sky is overcast with diffuse, soft lighting.

details.description: A long, symmetrical vista through a park lined with tall, dense green trees leads the eye toward a massive Art Deco building. The building, constructed from reddish-brown brick and stone, features a prominent central green copper dome and two flanking towers. The architectural style is a blend of Art Deco and Neo-Byzantine. In the foreground, there is a wide grassy lawn with manicured hedges and lamp posts along the tree lines. The sky is overcast with a soft, diffused light.

details.description: Symmetrical, long-axis view through a formal, tree-lined park toward a monumental brick-and-stone church crowned by a large green copper dome with smaller flanking cupolas. Ornate lampposts line the shaded allees on both sides, and clipped hedges and lawns frame the central perspective. The building at the end of the vista is the National Basilica of the Sacred Heart (Koekelberg Basilica), seen from within Parc Elisabeth in Brussels under an overcast sky.

entities.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart (Koekelberg Basilica) / Basilique Nationale du Sacré-Cœur / Nationale Basiliek van het Heilig-Hart
entities.notes: Belgium’s national basilica overlooking Parc Elisabeth in Koekelberg; monumental Art Deco church with a copper-clad dome. Foundation stone laid in 1905 under King Leopold II; principal architect Albert Van Huffel; construction resumed in the 1920s and was completed in 1970.

details.description: A straight, tree-lined boulevard in a city park leads the eye to a large church with a prominent green dome and ornate façade. The avenue is flanked by tall, trimmed trees and evenly spaced vintage lampposts, with well-kept lawns and hedges along the path. In the distance stands a monumental brick basilica with a central dome and arched entry, typical of a grand religious building in Brussels. The overall scene suggests a ceremonial approach to a major landmark, viewed from a low perspective along the central axis.

details.description: A long, symmetrical, tree‑lined park axis leads to a massive brick-and-stone basilica with a large central copper-clad dome and two tall flanking towers topped by smaller green domes. The facade shows strong Art Deco massing with broad arches and vertical ribs, combined with Neo‑Byzantine domes. This is the classic axial view from Elisabethpark toward the National Basilica of the Sacred Heart (Koekelberg) in Brussels. Overcast daylight, trimmed hedges and lawns frame the approach.

details.description: A historic European religious building with a prominent copper-green dome and accompanying smaller domes, set at the end of a tree-lined axis. The structure features a stone facade with arched windows and decorative details, typical of grand churches or cathedrals. It sits within a formal park or garden with trimmed hedges, a gravel/pathway, and greenery framing the approach. The sky is overcast, lending a muted light to the stone and patinaed copper surfaces. People can be seen along the edge of the park, adding scale to the monumental façade.

details.description: The image, which is oriented sideways (rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise), captures a distant view of the National Basilica of the Sacred Heart (Basilique Nationale du Sacré-Cœur) in Koekelberg, Brussels. The view is framed by the dense, mature trees of Elisabeth Park (Parc Elisabeth), creating a natural tunnel or avenue effect that leads the eye toward the monument. To the right (bottom in correct orientation) is a manicured green lawn. The basilica stands prominently in the background, showcasing its distinctive Art Deco and Neo-Byzantine architecture, characterized by a massive central green copper dome, flanking towers, and a facade constructed of brick and stone. The sky is overcast with a pale, cloudy layer.

details.description: A vertical shot looking down a long, tree-lined allee towards the National Basilica of the Sacred Heart in Koekelberg. The basilica is a massive Art Deco structure with a prominent green copper dome and two square towers. It is framed symmetrically by two rows of tall, dense green trees. In the foreground, there is a manicured green lawn and low-cut hedges. The sky is overcast with a soft, diffused light.

details.description: A long, straight, tree‑lined park axis leads to a monumental church with a large green copper dome and twin flanking towers. The facade shows broad arches and striped vertical detailing in an Art Deco style. The scene is viewed from a grassy promenade with hedges and paths under an overcast sky. This appears to be the Basilica of the Sacred Heart (Koekelberg Basilica) seen from Parc Elisabeth in Brussels, Belgium.
